turns.content: What brings you in today?

Hi, thanks for seeing me. I've had a sore throat and a dry, tickly cough for about four days now. My throat feels scratchy and it's painful to swallow, but not excruciating. I also noticed I've been feeling a bit run down and have a low-grade fever on and off, around 37.8°C. My roommate was diagnosed with the flu last week, so I thought I'd get checked just to be safe, even though I don't feel nearly as bad as she did.
When did this start?

It started about four days ago. I woke up with a mild scratchiness in my throat, and then the cough and fatigue developed over the next day or so. The low-grade fever came and went, mostly in the evenings.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

The discomfort is mainly centered in my throat, right around the back of my mouth and tonsil area. It feels scratchy and raw, like I've been shouting or something. When I swallow, it's a sharper pain, but it's not constant—just when I'm talking or eating. I also have a bit of a soreness in my upper chest from the coughing, but that's more of a dull ache and not the main issue.
Have you had any fever or chills?

Yes, I've had a low-grade fever. I checked a couple of times and it was around 37.8°C. I wouldn't say I've had full-on chills, but I did feel a bit achy and shivery in the evenings when the fever was up. No dramatic shaking or sweating, though.
What medications are you currently taking?

I'm not taking any medications at the moment. I usually avoid over-the-counter stuff unless I really need it, and so far I've just been resting and drinking warm tea. I don't have any prescription medications either.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
No, I don't have any chronic medical conditions. I'm generally pretty healthy and don't have any long-term issues.
Are you allergic to any medications?

Yes, I'm allergic to penicillin. I had a reaction to it when I was younger—got a rash and some swelling, so I've been careful to avoid it ever since. I don't know about other antibiotics, but I've never had issues with anything else.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

I've just been resting more than usual and drinking warm herbal tea with honey to soothe my throat. I also tried gargling with salt water a couple of times, but honestly, it didn't do much. I haven't taken any pain relievers or cold medicine since I wanted to see if it would pass on its own.
Does anything make it better or worse?

Warm drinks like tea definitely soothe my throat temporarily, and resting seems to help with the fatigue. On the other hand, talking a lot or swallowing dry foods makes the soreness worse. Cold air also seems to trigger my cough a bit more.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been a bit disruptive, honestly. I'm a university student, so I've had to skip a couple of lectures because I was too tired to focus, and the cough can be distracting during class. I'm still able to study and get my work done, but I feel more drained than usual and I've been going to bed earlier. It's not completely stopping me, but it's definitely not a normal week.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?

I don't smoke, never have. I do drink alcohol occasionally, maybe a glass of wine or a couple of drinks when I'm out with friends on weekends, but not during this illness obviously. I haven't had anything since the symptoms started.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
Not that I'm aware of. My roommate had the flu recently, but no one in my immediate family has been sick with anything similar in the past few weeks. They're all healthy as far as I know.
Have you experienced anything like this before?

I've had the occasional cold or mild sore throat before, but nothing that lasted this long with a fever and that dry cough. Usually, it clears up in a day or two, so this feels a bit more persistent, which is why I wanted to get it checked out.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?
Physically, I'd say around a 3 or 4 out of 10. The sore throat and cough are annoying and draining,
Is there anything else you think I should know?

Actually, one thing I forgot to mention—my appetite has been pretty low since this started. I'm not nauseous or anything, but the sore throat makes eating less appealing, and I've only been having light meals like soup. Also, I should mention that I was around my roommate a lot before she started showing symptoms, so the timing seems pretty suspicious for flu exposure.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I've been managing okay, honestly. I'm not too worried—I figured it was probably just a mild virus, but with my roommate having the flu, I wanted to be thorough. I've been taking it easy, staying hydrated, and catching up on sleep, so emotionally I feel pretty calm. It's more of an inconvenience than anything else, like I said.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I've been handling it pretty well—just taking it slow, drinking lots of fluids, and resting when I can. Emotionally, I'm not stressed or anxious; it's more of a mild annoyance. I think knowing my roommate had the flu and recovered fine helps keep things in perspective.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I've been managing by sticking to a routine—making sure I rest in the evenings, drinking warm fluids, and avoiding any heavy physical activity. I'm not panicking about it; I just want to make sure it's not something that needs treatment, like strep or the flu. Overall, I feel like my usual self, just a bit tired and scratchy-throated.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I've been coping by resting as much as my schedule allows and staying on top of fluids—lots of tea and water. I'm not avoiding normal life entirely, but I'm pacing myself. Emotionally, I'm totally fine; I know this is likely just a passing viral thing, so I'm not worried.
And how have you been coping with all of this?

I think I've already answered this a couple of times, but I'm still coping fine. I'm just resting, staying hydrated, and not pushing myself too hard. Emotionally, I'm not stressed about it—just a little tired and ready for it to pass.
